translated: The Pharisees came to him and asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any and every reason?” – They were testing him with this question, wanting to find a reason to accuse him.

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ka doo maa daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Zaaligu bɔ n nyɛ a?” Ka Yisa daa yɛli, “Ni, ‘Di ku ninsaala.’, ‘Di tum zina.’, ‘Di zu.’, ‘Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.’,
translated: And the man asked him, “Good teacher, what should I do?” And Jesus said, “Do not kill.”, “Do not commit adultery.”, “Do not steal.”, “Do not give false testimony against your neighbor.”,

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya puu maa daana bii la, ba daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, ‘Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u, ka mari u faari maa.’
translated: The elders gathered and saw that the goat was missing, but they did not tell the children, saying, ‘Let us see who took the goat away. If we tell them, they will hide it and we will not find it.’

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu la? - ni#:- ‘Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu; Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti.’ - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa?”
translated: And Jesus answered and said, “Did you not read what was spoken by God? - saying: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: U daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi nti booli ŋɔn pun zuusi seeba maa, ni, ba kyɛm na. Amaa ba daa bu sakki n‑kyɛ na.
translated: If someone gives you their thing and you don't use it well, they won't give it to you again. But if you use it well, they will give it to you again.

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.

translated: When the chief gives an order, and you do not do what he has told you, he will get angry and punish you accordingly.

translated: So; if someone says that Saddusidima - is not there, or, is not alive and here - do not believe it because you have seen him yourself.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.

translated: For God so loved the world that He gave His only Son, so that whoever believes in Him shall not perish.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.

translated: Woe to you scribes and Pharisees! You hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and the dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, ka kur kyɛ ka ninvugsuma dabɔya veela,
translated: Woe to you scribes and Pharisees! You hypocrites! You shut the door of the kingdom of heaven in people’s faces, but you yourselves do not enter, nor will you let those enter who are trying to.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.

translated: If you want to live well in this world, you must know yourself. Some people do not value themselves, but if a chief values himself, his people will also value him, so if you do not respect yourself, others will not respect you.

translated: If someone does not have food to eat, they do not invite others to eat with them; likewise, if someone does not have water, they do not invite others to drink with them.

text: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”
translated: If you do not prepare yourself well, you will not receive the blessings and mercy of God. And you will not be able to overcome difficulties when they come.

translated: If someone does something good for you and you thank them, it is God who will reward them. But if someone does something bad to you and you forgive them, it is God who will reward you.

text: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”
